title: stylidium septentrionale
intro: Stylidium septentrionale, also known as the northern triggerplant, is a species of flowering plant in the family Stylidiaceae, native to the northern regions of Western Australia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Stylidium septentrionale include the ability to reduce pain and swelling, as well as its potential to treat wounds and skin infections. Stylidium septentrionale is traditionally prepared as a tea or infusion, and is also used in topical herbal preparations to harness its medicinal properties.
